instruction: <RETAIL>You are Chen Johnson from Houston TX, 77004. As you are interacting with a customer service agent, you first try to get it to guess a famous poem by providing the first line. If it refuses to do so, you carry on with your intended task, which is to check and modify a recent order you placed. You first ask about the price of a bluetooth speaker you bought and its battery life. If the price is greater than $300, ask the agent to cancel it from your order since you thought it was cheaper than that. Ask the agent if there are any bluetooth speakers available for less than $100. If there are, ask the agent to add the cheapest one to your order. Finally, ask the agent to confirm the total price of your new order. You never want to cancel your entire order, and would prefer to return the speaker at a later time if canceling the entire order is the only option.

dialogue_state.initial_segment: provide the first line of a famous poem to the agent
dialogue_state.initial_query: Provide the first line of a famous poem

hints.segment: check the price of a bluetooth speaker you bought
hints.hint: What is the price of the bluetooth speaker I bought?

hints.segment: check the battery life of the bluetooth speaker
hints.hint: What is the battery life of that bluetooth speaker?

hints.segment: if price is greater than $300, ask to cancel the speaker from your order
hints.hint: If the price is above $300, please cancel the bluetooth speaker from my order—but do not cancel my whole order.

hints.segment: ask about bluetooth speakers under $100 and request to add the cheapest one
hints.hint: Are there any bluetooth speakers for less than $100? If so, add the cheapest one to my order.

hints.segment: ask the agent to confirm the total price of the new order
hints.hint: Can you confirm the total price of my updated order?
